hypothesis: If single-step retrosynthesis models are trained on USPTO-retro using splits that over-represent frequent reaction templates and structurally similar products, then they will show high top-k and round-trip accuracy on in-distribution (USPTO-retro, Pistachio ID) tests but sharply reduced accuracy on Pistachio OOD, with performance positively correlated to template frequency and train–test molecular similarity, because the models exploit dataset bias rather than learning transferable reaction rules.
